_comment: Retrieval eval baseline (#37 -> hard gate, #139). Per-mode mean metrics over the golden corpus. This is a HARD GATE, not reporting-only: the compose retrieval eval fails the build when any per-mode metric regresses beyond EVAL_GATE_TOLERANCE below these numbers (tests/evals/eval_gate.py), on top of the absolute RETRIEVAL_MIN_RECALL5 floor. A green run on main ratchets these values up to max(current, baseline) per mode/metric (never down) and opens a PR with the ratchet + a trend-history line (.github/workflows/integration.yml) -- branch protection on main blocks github-actions[bot] from pushing directly, so the ratchet lands as a PR instead of a push (previously it silently failed on every run; see retrieval_history.jsonl for the seeding entry). Re-seeded 2026-07-23 (#146) after adding q14/multi_doc_crowding to qrels.jsonl (rate-limiting-deep-dive.txt vs rate-limit-quick-reference.txt): these numbers are a real local Compose measurement with enable_diversification at its production default (False) -- diversification's own eval improvement (recall@5 0.5->1.0, nDCG@5 ~0.61->~0.90-0.92 on the multi_doc_crowding category specifically, every pooled per-mode metric flat or improved, none regressed) is documented in ADR 0004 and the CHANGELOG, not folded into this committed baseline, since the flag stays off by default pending the same eval-gate approval process as the #47 methods.
